---
title: "How-to guides (planned)"
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Planned area: not yet written.** The conceptual guides (e.g.,
[CoverageJSON Uncovered](../formats/coveragejson.qmd)) link forward to the
task-focused how-to guides below. Each entry is a placeholder for a recipe that
walks through a complete, runnable example for a reader who already has the
concepts; the anchors are stable so the guides' "Going further" links resolve
as the how-tos land.
:::

## Serving & consuming a TiledNdArray {#sec-howto-tiled-ndarray}

How an `NdArray` range is split into a `TileSet`: `tileShape`, `urlTemplate`,
and why a single array may carry **multiple tile sets** (e.g., spatial vs.
temporal chunking) so a client can pick the layout that matches its access
pattern. Bridges the conceptual model to the same tiling ideas behind Zarr and
Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF.

## External references & lazy loading {#sec-howto-external-refs}

Fetching a coverage's `domain` and `ranges` on demand from URLs rather than
inlining them, and how this composes with serving coverages through
**OGC API - Coverages**. Covers the "two levels of fetch-on-demand" (the
coverage document, then its referenced range data).

## Non-standard domains & custom axes {#sec-howto-custom-axes}

Working with coverages that have **no standard `domainType`**: defining custom
axes for cases the built-in domain types do not cover, such as ensemble members
or hyperspectral bands.

## Vector quantities with parameterGroups {#sec-howto-vectors}

Using `parameterGroups` to bind related parameters into a single physical
quantity (the canonical example being wind as `u`/`v` components) and how a
client reads a group back as one vector field.
